  "findings": [
    "The calculated tension per sling leg is approximately 2,309.4 lbs, which is well below the rated working load limit (WLL) of 3,500 lbs per leg."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Proceed with the planned lift using the specified rigging configuration."
  ],
  "rationale": "For a symmetric two-leg sling arrangement, the tension in each leg is calculated as: Tension = (Load / 2) / sin(angle). Here, Tension = (4,000 lbs / 2) / sin(60 degrees) = 2,000 / 0.866025 = 2,309.4 lbs. Since 2,309.4 lbs is less than the 3,500 lbs vertical WLL rating of each leg, the configuration is safe and compliant.",
  "references": [
    "ASME B30.9",
    "OSHA 1926.251"
  ]
}
